report_name: FulcrumSec Ransomware Group Claims Attack on Colombian Healthcare Firm IMEVI

threat_actors.name: FulcrumSec
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group that claimed responsibility for a double-extortion cyberattack against the Colombian company IMEVI. The group listed the victim on its data leak site on May 1, 2026, threatening to publish stolen data if a ransom was not paid.
threat_actors.goals: To extort a ransom payment from the victim, IMEVI, by encrypting their data and threatening to leak it publicly.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: As part of a double-extortion attack, FulcrumSec encrypted files on IMEVI's systems to disrupt operations and demand a ransom.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The attack followed a double-extortion model, which involved FulcrumSec exfiltrating sensitive data from IMEVI's network before encryption to threaten its public release on their leak site.

malware.name: FulcrumSec Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload used by the FulcrumSec group to encrypt victim systems and facilitate double-extortion attacks.

identities.name: IMEVI
identities.description: A Colombian company specializing in healthcare equipment and engineering services that was targeted by the FulcrumSec ransomware group in a data breach incident.

infrastructure.name: FulcrumSec Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A data leak site used by the FulcrumSec ransomware group to publicly name their victims and threaten the release of stolen data. IMEVI was listed on this site on May 1, 2026.
